title: Glazed Vietnamese Soybean Sprout Tagine
description: A briny, crispy dish featuring soybean sprouts and kongnamul cooked in a traditional clay tajine, finished with a sweet‑savory glaze that makes for a satisfying solo dinner.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a ttukbaegi over medium heat.
Add garlic and ginger and sauté until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Stir in carrot slices and cook for another 3 minutes.
Add the soybean sprouts and kongnamul, searing them lightly for 4 minutes.
Pour in soy sauce, rice vinegar, water, and a pinch of salt; bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot and let the mixture cook for 8 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Reduce the heat, uncover, and simmer until the liquid has thickened into a glaze, about 5 minutes.
Finish with toasted sesame seeds and a drizzle of sesame oil before serving.
